county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1844; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); Central Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Marion County Clerk (Ocala). Formed 1844 from Alachua, Mosquito (now Orange), and Hillsborough. Named for Revolutionary War hero Francis Marion. Ocala was a center of horse breeding and the citrus industry. Silver Springs area. Marion County Historical Society has local materials. Fort King (Seminole Wars) records at NARA. Significant enslaved population on antebellum plantations. Ocala National Forest area homesteading records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1925; 20th-century formation; South Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Martin County Clerk (Stuart). Formed 1925 from Palm Beach and St. Lucie — one of Florida's youngest counties. Pre-1925 records in parent counties. Stuart Heritage Museum has local materials. Indian River area citrus and fishing industry records. House of Refuge at Gilbert's Bar (1876). Jonathan Dickinson State Park area. Very sparse population before the 20th century. Florida East Coast Railway records important.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1836; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); South Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Miami-Dade County Clerk (Miami). Originally Dade County (formed 1836 from Monroe), renamed Miami-Dade 1997. Named for Major Francis Dade (killed in Seminole Wars). Miami was incorporated 1896 with arrival of Flagler's railroad. HistoryMiami Museum has extensive collections. Vizcaya Museum and Gardens. Significant Cuban, Haitian, and Caribbean immigrant records (1960s+). Miccosukee Tribe of Indians of Florida has presence here. Pre-1836 records in Monroe. Homestead area agricultural records.

county_metadata.record_losses: 1886 Key West fire destroyed some county records; additional losses from hurricane damage
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1823; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); South Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Monroe County Clerk (Key West). Formed 1823 from St. Johns — one of Florida's oldest counties. BURNED COUNTY — 1886 Key West fire destroyed some county records and additional hurricane damage over the years. Key West was Florida's largest city in the 19th century. Key West Art and Historical Society. Wrecking (salvage) industry records. Cuban cigar-making community records. Bahamian community (Conchs) records. Overseas Railroad (Flagler) records. Fort Jefferson/Dry Tortugas records at NARA. Multi-ethnic heritage.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1824; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); North Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Nassau County Clerk (Fernandina Beach). Formed 1824 from Duval. Amelia Island has been under 8 flags — French, Spanish, British, Patriots, Green Cross, Mexican, Confederate, and US. Fernandina Beach was a major 19th-century port. Nassau County Historical Society (Amelia Island Museum of History) has excellent collections. Fort Clinch State Park records. Pre-1824 records in Duval and St. Johns. Timber and shipping industry.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1915; 20th-century formation; Panhandle region
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Okaloosa County Clerk (Crestview). Formed 1915 from Santa Rosa and Walton. Pre-1915 records in parent counties. Eglin Air Force Base (one of the largest military installations in the world) and Hurlburt Field generate military records. Fort Walton Beach area. Heritage Museum of Northwest Florida (Valparaiso) has local collections. Choctawhatchee Bay area settlement records. Named from Choctaw words meaning 'pleasant place.'

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1917; 20th-century formation; Central Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Okeechobee County Clerk (Okeechobee). Formed 1917 from Osceola, Palm Beach, and St. Lucie. Pre-1917 records in parent counties. Lake Okeechobee area. 1928 Okeechobee Hurricane devastated the area — some local records lost. Okeechobee Historical Society has local materials. Cattle ranching and sugar cane industry. Brighton Seminole Reservation nearby. Very sparse population before 20th-century drainage projects.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1824; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); Central Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Orange County Clerk (Orlando). Originally Mosquito County (formed 1824 from St. Johns), renamed Orange 1845. One of Florida's older counties. Orlando area citrus and cattle heritage preceded Disney World (1971). Orange County Regional History Center has extensive genealogical collections. Pre-1824 records in St. Johns. Fort Gatlin (Seminole Wars) records at NARA. Parent county for Seminole (1913), Osceola (1887), and others. Significant African American community records — Eatonville (first incorporated Black municipality in the US, 1887).

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1887; post-Civil War formation; Central Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Osceola County Clerk (Kissimmee). Formed 1887 from Brevard and Orange. Pre-1887 records in parent counties. Kissimmee cattle country heritage. Osceola County Historical Society and Pioneer Village has local materials. Named for Seminole leader Osceola. Kissimmee River area. St. Cloud area — significant G.A.R. (Grand Army of the Republic) colony founded 1909 by Union veterans. Disney World development (1960s+) transformed the area.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1909; 20th-century formation; South Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Palm Beach County Clerk (West Palm Beach). Formed 1909 from Dade (now Miami-Dade). Pre-1909 records in Dade. Henry Flagler's development of Palm Beach (1890s) shaped the county. Historical Society of Palm Beach County has extensive collections. Whitehall (Flagler Museum). Pahokee and Belle Glade area — agricultural migrant worker records. African American community in West Palm Beach's northwest neighborhood. Lake Okeechobee area agricultural records.
